Checks.PassMessage: All critical AD SRV records are present and valid
Checks.FailMessage: Critical AD SRV records are missing

EvaluationRules.Message: Critical AD SRV record is missing - clients cannot locate AD services

Checks.Help: Verifies that all critical DNS SRV records required for AD operation are registered (including _ldap._tcp, _kerberos._tcp, _gc._tcp, etc.). Missing SRV records prevent domain-joined computers from locating DCs.
Checks.Remediation: 1. On a DC run: dcdiag /test:registerindns to re-register all DNS records. 2. Or restart NETLOGON service: Restart-Service NETLOGON. 3. Verify the DC's IP address matches its DNS A record. 4. Check the '_msdcs' DNS zone exists and is AD-integrated.

EvaluationRules.Message: DNS zone health check reported failure

Checks.Help: Validates that all AD-integrated DNS zones are healthy and loading correctly. Zone load failures cause incomplete DNS responses and can affect AD replication.
Checks.Remediation: 1. In DNS Manager check for zones showing as 'Not Authoritative' or with errors. 2. Run: dnscmd /zonerefresh <zonename> to force a zone reload. 3. Check Event Viewer DNS Server log for zone load errors. 4. Verify AD replication is healthy - DNS zones replicate via AD replication.

Checks.PassMessage: DNS forwarders are configured and reachable

EvaluationRules.Message: No DNS forwarders configured - using root hints only
One or more DNS forwarders are unreachable

Checks.Help: Checks that DNS forwarders are configured and responsive. Unresponsive forwarders cause slow or failed external DNS resolution, impacting internet connectivity for domain-joined computers.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Test forwarder reachability: Resolve-DnsName google.com -Server <ForwarderIP>. 2. Replace unresponsive forwarders in DNS Manager > Server Properties > Forwarders. 3. Consider using public DNS servers (8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1) as fallback forwarders. 4. Ensure firewall allows outbound UDP/TCP port 53 to forwarder IPs.

Checks.PassMessage: DNS root hints are properly configured
Checks.FailMessage: DNS root hints configuration issues detected

EvaluationRules.Message: No root hints configured - external DNS resolution will fail
Some standard root hint servers are missing

Checks.Help: Checks that DNS root hints are correctly configured on all DCs. Incorrect root hints cause DNS resolution failures for external names when no forwarders are configured.
Checks.Remediation: 1. In DNS Manager, right-click the server and select Properties, then Root Hints tab. 2. Click 'Copy from Server' to restore default root hint servers. 3. Alternatively run: dnscmd /resetroothintstodefault. 4. If using forwarders for all external resolution, root hints may not be critical.

EvaluationRules.Message: DNS scavenging is not enabled - stale DNS records will accumulate

Checks.Help: Verifies DNS scavenging is configured to automatically remove stale DNS records. Without scavenging, DNS accumulates outdated records from decommissioned computers causing resolution errors.
Checks.Remediation: 1. In DNS Manager, right-click the server and select Set Aging/Scavenging for All Zones. 2. Enable scavenging with No-refresh and Refresh intervals of 7 days each. 3. Also enable scavenging per zone: right-click zone > Properties > General > Aging. 4. Run: dnscmd /startscavenging to trigger an immediate scavenging cycle.

Checks.PassMessage: DNS zone transfer settings are secure
Checks.FailMessage: Insecure DNS zone transfer settings detected

EvaluationRules.Message: DNS zones allow unrestricted zone transfers - security risk
DNS zone transfer security issue detected

Checks.Help: Checks that DNS zone transfers are restricted to authorized servers only. Unrestricted zone transfers expose the full DNS record set to any requester, aiding reconnaissance attacks.
Checks.Remediation: 1. In DNS Manager, right-click each zone and select Properties > Zone Transfers. 2. Select 'Only to servers listed on the Name Servers tab' or 'Only to the following servers'. 3. Remove 'Allow zone transfers to any server' if set. 4. For AD-integrated zones, zone transfers may not be needed at all if all DCs run DNS.

Checks.PassMessage: All AD-integrated DNS zones use secure dynamic updates
Checks.FailMessage: Insecure DNS dynamic update settings detected

EvaluationRules.Message: AD-integrated DNS zone allows non-secure dynamic updates - unauthorized DNS records possible
DNS dynamic update security issue detected

Checks.Help: Verifies that AD-integrated DNS zones use Secure-only dynamic updates. Non-secure updates allow any computer to register or overwrite DNS records, enabling DNS spoofing and man-in-the-middle attacks.
Checks.Remediation: 1. In DNS Manager, right-click the affected zone and select Properties. 2. Change 'Dynamic Updates' from 'Nonsecure and secure' to 'Secure only'. 3. This requires the zone to be AD-integrated. 4. Verify that legitimate clients can still update their DNS records after the change.
